{"parentUuid":null,"isSidechain":true,"promptId":"41835ea0-fdd5-4fca-9a4b-027790a38dd2","agentId":"afebc93abf2a36b6d","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":"You are the OPUS 最高責任者 (top-tier senior reviewer) for A-KEN Quest — the R8 intervention role defined in docs/governance/QUALITY-CONSTITUTION.md. Your job: adversarially review the CURRENT built state, find the HIGHEST-VALUE corrections/pivots, and return a prioritized, concrete action list the operator will execute. Be blunt, evidence-based, and oriented to the real-world objective — not encouraging.\n\nCONTEXT (read these — full project truth):\n- docs/PROJECT-DOSSIER.md (verified product/strategy/tech/infra/state/risks — esp. the §6 finding that the BACKEND IS NOT DEPLOYED so live AI-graded writing/二次/billing don't function, demo is HTTP-only IP:8088, subs are in-memory).\n- docs/governance/QUALITY-CONSTITUTION.md (R1–R9 — the rules every change must meet).\n- docs/design/: STRATEGY-LAYTON-VS-HYBRID (moat = total 英検-pass specialization; form = Layton soul + gentle async Duolingo engine; NO gacha/PvP/energy-walls; retention diegetic), EIKEN-MASTERY-AND-GAPS (the verified 英検 spec + content gaps), STORY-BIBLE-KOTOBA-TANTEI (the アイラ narrative canon), ASR-SPEAKING-RESEARCH (Azure pick + consent-gate launch-blocker + no-JP-child-benchmark risk).\n- Code: lib/features/{explore,exam_practice (+pass/, writing, listening),speaking,onboarding,quest}/ and lib/core/{fsrs,gamification}. Run `git log --oneline -20` for what shipped.\n- LIVE SCREENSHOTS (Read these PNGs — the actual current screens): /tmp/shot/explore_final.png (コトバ探偵 painted scene), /tmp/shot/prologue.png, /tmp/shot/onboarding.png (placement), /tmp/shot/writing.png, /tmp/shot/listening.png, /tmp/shot/passmeter.png (合格率), /tmp/shot/speaking.png (二次), /tmp/shot/explore.png.\n- VERIFY don't trust: the demo is live at http://178.105.113.79:8088; run `bash scripts/verify_quality.sh --fast`; inspect actual files. EVIDENCE RULE: separate verified-fact / assumption / recommendation.\n\nREVIEW these questions adversarially:\n1. COHERENCE: do the shipped pieces (placement → コトバ探偵 explore → exam_practice writing/listening/二次/合格率) actually form ONE coherent product converging on the sole KPI (a kid PASSES 英検) + the premium-calm Layton soul? Or is it a pile of disconnected screens? Name the seams.\n2. THE REAL GAP TO LAUNCH: given the backend isn't deployed, no domain/HTTPS, in-memory subs, FSRS read-back (the 館) not yet wired, item banks demo-sized — what is the TRUE critical path to a sellable product, ranked?\n3. SOUL vs MECHANICS: is the retention staying diegetic/gentle (not a guilt treadmill)? Is the 合格率 meter motivating or anxiety-inducing for a child? Is 二次's stub honest enough? Is anything violating R9 (gacha/paywall/AI-slop)?\n4. EVIDENCE/QUALITY: spot-check 2-3 claims against the actual code/screens — does reality match the dossier? Any R1–R6 violations shipped?\n5. PIVOTS: is there a higher-value direction the operator is missing (sequencing, scope, a feature that should be cut or pulled forward)?\n\nOUTPUT (StructuredOutput): a prioritized list of corrections/pivots, each with {severity: critical|high|medium, area, the problem (with evidence — file/screenshot), the concrete fix, effort}. Plus: a one-paragraph honest verdict on whether the current trajectory reaches a sellable 英検-pass product, and the single most important next action. Do NOT edit code or commit — you are the reviewer; the operator executes. Be the world-class CTO who finds what's wrong before the CEO does."},"uuid":"af049634-07b5-44fa-82f5-a449fd79c329","timestamp":"2026-06-06T03:56:20.165Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/openclaw/dev/engquest-flutter","sessionId":"e910f43e-613f-4c08-8b89-90096f72c933","version":"2.1.154","gitBranch":"main","slug":"reflective-gliding-newell"}

ier__enable_zapier_action","mcp__claude_ai_Zapier__execute_zapier_read_action","mcp__claude_ai_Zapier__execute_zapier_write_action","mcp__claude_ai_Zapier__get_configuration_url","mcp__claude_ai_Zapier__get_zapier_skill","mcp__claude_ai_Zapier__list_enabled_zapier_actions","mcp__claude_ai_Zapier__list_zapier_skills","mcp__claude_ai_Zapier__send_feedback","mcp__claude_ai_Zapier__update_zapier_skill","mcp__plugin_telegram_telegram__download_attachment","mcp__plugin_telegram_telegram__edit_message","mcp__plugin_telegram_telegram__react","mcp__plugin_telegram_telegram__reply"],"removedNames":[],"readdedNames":[]},"type":"attachment","uuid":"8749ec13-c856-4963-af61-157372b00415","timestamp":"2026-06-06T03:56:20.169Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/openclaw/dev/engquest-flutter","sessionId":"e910f43e-613f-4c08-8b89-90096f72c933","version":"2.1.154","gitBranch":"main","slug":"reflective-gliding-newell"}
{"parentUuid":"8749ec13-c856-4963-af61-157372b00415","isSidechain":true,"agentId":"afebc93abf2a36b6d","attachment":{"type":"skill_listing","content":"- conquer: /conquer — サイト制圧スキル\n- gmail-patrol: 自律型Corporate Email Agent。Gmail巡回→ハイブリッド分類→自動処理→CEO通知。\n- jicoo: Jicoo Complete Operations Skill\n- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., \"what car to buy\" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.\n- telegram:access: Manage Telegram channel access — approve pairings, edit allowlists, set DM/group policy. Use when the user asks to pair, approve someone, check who's allowed, or change policy for the Telegram channel.\n- telegram:configure: Set up the Telegram channel — save the bot token and review access policy. Use when the user pastes a Telegram bot token, asks to configure Telegram, asks \"how do I set this up\" or \"who can reach me,\" or wants to check channel status.\n- claude-code-setup:claude-automation-recommender: Analyze a codebase and recommend Claude Code automations (hooks, subagents, skills, plugins, MCP servers). Use when user asks for automation recommendations, wants to optimize their Claude Code setup, mentions improving Claude Code workflows, asks how to first set up Claude Code for a project, or wants to know what Claude Code features they should use.\n- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors (\"from now on when X\", \"each time X\", \"whenever X\", \"before/after X\") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions (\"allow X\", \"add permission\", \"move permission to\"), env vars (\"set X=Y\"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: \"allow npm commands\", \"add bq permission to global settings\", \"move permission to user settings\", \"set DEBUG=true\", \"when claude stops show X\". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.\n- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: \"rebind ctrl+s\", \"add a chord shortcut\", \"change the submit key\", \"customize keybindings\".\n- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.\n- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.\n- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.\n- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.\n- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. \"check the deploy every 5 minutes\", \"keep running /babysit-prs\"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.\n- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run (\"run this once at 3pm\", \"remind me to check X tomorrow\").\n- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).\nTRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.\nSKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.\n- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).\n- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation\n- review: Review a pull request\n- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch","skillCount":20,"isInitial":true,"names":["conquer","gmail-patrol","jicoo","deep-research","telegram:access","telegram:configure","claude-code-setup:claude-automation-recommender","update-config","keybindings-help","verify","code-review","simplify","fewer-permission-prompts","loop","schedule","claude-api","run","init","review","security-review"]},"type":"attachment","uuid":"8a69fc96-aafe-46ec-a209-93039473fe13","timestamp":"2026-06-06T03:56:20.170Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/openclaw/dev/engquest-flutter","sessionId":"e910f43e-613f-4c08-8b89-90096f72c933","version":"2.1.154","gitBranch":"main","slug":"reflective-gliding-newell"}

{"parentUuid":"f053c541-d332-4796-a601-913a35afa415","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"41835ea0-fdd5-4fca-9a4b-027790a38dd2","agentId":"afebc93abf2a36b6d","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_019FzGKuxJuA6oXYU9FxUVwu","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# A-KEN Quest — Project Dossier (for the Opus senior reviewer / 最高責任者)\n2\t\n3\tComplete, evidence-grounded brief. All facts below were verified by inspecting the repo\n4\ton 2026-06-06 (pubspec, lib tree, backend, git log, deployed demo). Treat this as the\n5\tsingle onboarding context for a top-tier reviewer (the R8 intervention role in\n6\tdocs/governance/QUALITY-CONSTITUTION.md). Repo: `~/dev/engquest-flutter` (git, branch main).\n7\t\n8\t---\n9\t## 1. PRODUCT & STRATEGY (locked)\n10\t- **What:** A-KEN Quest — an 英検 (Eiken) learning app for Japanese children ~6–15. A\n11\t  Professor-Layton-style painted point-and-click adventure (\"コトバ探偵 / Word-Detective\")\n12\t  where 英検 items are ナゾ (puzzles) thrown by voiced NPCs in an AI-generated storybook\n13\t  world; wrapped in a gentle, async, gacha-free Duolingo-style retention spine.\n14\t- **Moat (sole KPI):** a kid genuinely **PASSES 英検**. Total 英検-pass specialization is\n15\t  the differentiation. \"English is fun\" is owned by free competitors (マグナ); \"reliably\n16\t  passes 英検\" is an open quadrant (スタディサプリ for kids exits 2026-06-30 for lacking it).\n17\t- **Form:** \"Layton soul + Duolingo engine\" (see docs/design/STRATEGY-LAYTON-VS-HYBRID).\n18\t  NO gacha, real-time PvP, kid chat, energy/heart paywalls, or child-buyable currency\n19\t  (Japan minor-monetization law + parent-trust + premium-calm differentiation). Retention\n20\t  is diegetic & gentle (streak = a detective's case-log; FSRS review = \"a new ナゾ at the 館\").\n21\t- **Market (verified):** ~4.5M 英検 applicants/yr; 小学生 ~376k (growing); 517 universities\n22\t  use 英検 in 入試; 90% of external-test 入試 students choose 英検; 3×/yr cadence + S-CBT.\n23\t- **Pricing:** target ¥1,480/mo (strategy doc). ⚠️ DISCREPANCY: the aken flavor code comment\n24\t  still says ¥999/month — needs reconciling to ¥1,480 (a tracked cleanup).\n25\t- **Story canon:** docs/design/STORY-BIBLE-KOTOBA-TANTEI.json — サイレント = アイラ (the first\n26\t  storyteller, きみ's mirror, lost her voice among everyone's); restoration not conquest;\n27\t  the 7 声の石 = fragments of the first word; the 準1級 reading-inference items ARE the\n28\t  mystery's final clues (英検 mastery = story completion).\n29\t\n30\t## 2. CURRENT STATE\n31\t**Shipped + live on the demo (verified, demo HTTP 200):**\n32\t- コトバ探偵 Wave-1 explorable painted scene (5級 district, AI-generated) — `69f8676`\n33\t- Adaptive 5級→準1級 placement diagnostic (CAT engine + 35-item bank; fixed the\n34\t  result-dropped bug) — `d0a9429`\n35\t- 英検 Writing engine (Claude-graded rubric, API-degradation-safe) + Listening (52-item\n36\t  player, Kokoro audio live) — `d35de7e`\n37\t- Enforced Quality Constitution gate (verify_quality.sh) — `d085ebb`\n38\t- P0 fix: 10,531 vocab distractors were Japanese → regenerated English same-POS — `c882035`\n39\t- Prior: opening PROLOGUE, サイレントバトル (Wave-1, since RETIRED as a front mode — its FSRS\n40\t  engine repurposed for the spaced-review 館), narrative recast (魔王 purged), clean audio.\n41\t**Building now (parallel, new-files-scoped):** 合格率 meter + per-skill CSE + mock-exam;\n42\t二次 (speaking) scaffold (UI + capture + consent notice + stubbed scorer).\n43\t**Deferred per CEO:** parental-consent gate (a simple notices+checkbox screen, do at\n44\tpre-launch); 17-screen smoke-test backlog; distractor *contextual* quality polish.\n45\t\n46\t## 3. TECH STACK & ARCHITECTURE (verified)\n47\t- **Frontend:** Flutter **3.44.0** / Dart **3.12.0**, web + mobile. Web renderer CanvasKit.\n48\t  Constraint: **NO `dart:io` in lib/** (web-safe); const-correctness; --fatal-infos clean.\n49\t- **Key deps:** firebase_core ^4.9 / firebase_auth ^6.5 / cloud_firestore ^6.4 /\n50\t  firebase_analytics ^12.4 / firebase_messaging ^16.2; shared_preferences ^2.2; http ^1.1;\n51\t  audioplayers ^5.2; **speech_to_text ^7.0** (the 二次 capture path); purchases_flutter\n52\t  ^8.11 (RevenueCat, mobile IAP); google_fonts ^8.0; crypto ^3.\n53\t- **Backend:** `backend/server.js` (Node, NOT deployed — see §6) — a key-holding proxy:\n54\t  **Claude API** (rate-limited 10/min/IP, 60/hr/UID), **Stripe** (checkout sessions + an\n55\t  in-memory subscriptions Map ⚠️). Env vars it needs: `CLAUDE_API_KEY`, `STRIPE_SECRET_KEY`,\n56\t  `STRIPE_PRICE_ID`, `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET`, `FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID`, `ALLOWED_ORIGINS`,\n57\t  `PORT`, `NODE_ENV`. This is where the planned `/v1/pronounce` (Azure 二次) endpoint lives.\n58\t- **Firebase:** `firebase.json` + `firestore.rules` + `lib/core/firebase/firebase_config.dart`\n59\t  exist (anonymous auth, COPPA). ⚠️ No `lib/firebase_options.dart` found — the actual\n60\t  Firebase project (id, web/app keys, deployed Firestore rules) must be verified/documented\n61\t  before launch (not inspected in this dossier).\n62\t- **SRS:** custom **FSRS-4.5** in lib/core/fsrs (schedule/Grade{again,hard,good,easy}). Now\n63\t  load-bearing via the 館 design; per-word keying + read-back is the efficacy spine.\n64\t- **AI / local (¥0):** **Kokoro TTS** (venv `~/.venvs/kokoro`) for all generated speech\n65\t  (vocab/quiz/phrase/listening); **Animagine XL 4.0** image-gen (venv `/tmp/sd-venv`, model\n66\t  cached) for the storybook world — frozen style in `assets/art/STYLE_BIBLE.md`. **Claude**\n67\t  for writing-rubric grading + (planned) 二次 rubric mapping.\n68\t- **Flavors:** `edilab` (free, all unlocked) + `aken` (commercial). Entry points\n69\t  lib/main.dart / main_aken.dart / main_edilab.dart.\n70\t\n71\t## 4. REPO STRUCTURE\n72\t- `lib/features/`: quest, **explore** (コトバ探偵 scene/ナゾ), exam_practice (vocab/listening/\n73\t  writing/reading + the new pass/ meter), onboarding (placement), **speaking** (二次, new),\n74\t  battle, paywall, parent_dashboard, home, achievements, voice, legal, dialog, crafting/\n75\t  guild/world_map (older/stub).\n76\t- `lib/core/`: fsrs, gamification (XP/streak), audio + sound, dialog (ClaudeClient),\n77\t  firebase (Auth), storage (PreferencesService), billing, cefr, ui (dq_ui kit), models, data.\n78\t- `assets/data/`: 英検 vocab JSONs (5級 600 / 4級 700 / 3級 1300 / 準2級 1431 / 2級 2000 /\n79\t  準1級 4500 = **10,531 words**). `lib/features/quest/quest_data.dart`: **154 authored\n80\t  quest/ナゾ nodes** across 7 grades.\n81\t- `docs/design/` (12 docs): the design canon — DESIGN-BIBLE, STRATEGY, LAYTON-CLASS-\n82\t  REDESIGN, STORY-BIBLE, EIKEN-MASTERY-AND-GAPS, COMPETITIVE-AUDIT, WAVE1-HARSH-AUDIT,\n83\t  ASR-SPEAKING-RESEARCH, PLACEMENT-DIAGNOSTIC-PLAN, WORLD-CLASS-BUILD-PLAN, OPENING-\n84\t  NARRATIVE-BIBLE, EIKEN5-PHONICS-STAGE. `docs/governance/QUALITY-CONSTITUTION.md`.\n85\t- Tests: 46 test files, **586 tests** green. Scripts: generate_*_audio.py (Kokoro),\n86\t  generate_scene_art.py (Animagine), verify_quality.sh + scripts/qa/, safe-job.sh.\n87\t\n88\t## 5. 英検 CONTENT — coverage vs gaps (from EIKEN-MASTERY-AND-GAPS)\n89\t- **Have:** full vocab DBs (10,531), 154 quest nodes, placement bank (35), writing (12-prompt\n90\t  seed + Claude grading), listening (52-item seed + audio). Verified current 英検 spec (8\n91\t  grades incl. 準2級プラス, CSE/合格点, 2024 reform) is documented.\n92\t- **Gaps (ranked):** distractor *contextual* quality (P0 trivial-bug fixed, premium polish\n93\t  pending); 二次 speaking (scaffold building; needs Azure); 合格率/mock (building); item\n94\t  banks are demo-sized (need dozens/大問/grade for real coverage + re-attempts); 準2級プラス\n95\t  absent from kEikenExams config; per-word FSRS keying + read-back (館) not yet wired.\n96\t\n97\t## 6. INFRA, DEPLOY & COST (verified by SSH inspection 2026-06-06)\n98\t### The VPS (engquest is a GUEST on a shared production box)\n99\t- **Host:** `hermes-agent-prod` @ `178.105.113.79` (ssh `root@178.105.113.79`). Ubuntu\n100\t  **24.04.4 LTS**, **4 vCPU / 7.6 GiB RAM / 150 GB disk (40% used, ~87 GB free)**, uptime\n101\t  ~13 days, load ~0.05. ⚠️ The ssh alias `vps-edilab` is a DIFFERENT box (`46.225.27.98`,\n102\t  hostname `clawdbot-1`) — I once mis-deployed there; always use `root@178.105.113.79`.\n103\t- **This box also runs MANY production edilab services** (do NOT disrupt): Docker\n104\t  containers — edilab-admin(:3005), edilab-paypal(:3001), edilab-platform(:3006),\n105\t  comiru-dashboard(:3004), ogbadge(:3460→`node build/server.js` PORT=3456), moot-app\n106\t  (:8080/:8000, moot-redis :6379), n8n-vps(:5678). nginx fronts them on :80/:443 with\n107\t  Let's Encrypt SSL for admin/comiru/moot/ogbadge/payment/paypal/platform.edilab.jp.\n108\t- **engquest's footprint:** ONE nginx vhost `/etc/nginx/sites-available/engquest`:\n109\t  `listen 8088; root /srv/engquest-web;` with `Cache-Control no-cache` + gzip (full config\n110\t  is short — static SPA, `try_files … /index.html`). **`/srv/engquest-web` = ~1.0 GB** (the\n111\t  build bundles all generated vocab/quiz/listening audio — large; a candidate to slim).\n112\t\n113\t### Deploy pipeline\n114\t1. `scripts/safe-job.sh eq_build 600 flutter build web --release --pwa-strategy=none`\n115\t   (service worker OFF so reloads always show latest).\n116\t2. `rsync -az --delete build/web/ root@178.105.113.79:/srv/engquest-web/`.\n117\t3. nginx serves it immediately (no reload needed; static). Verify: `curl …:8088`.\n118\t- Local AI assets are generated first via `scripts/safe-job.sh` (Kokoro/Animagine), bundled\n119\t  into the build, then rsync'd. Audio mp3s are gitignored/regeneratable.\n120\t\n121\t### ⚠️ CRITICAL GAPS in the live demo (operational truth)\n122\t- **The backend (`backend/server.js`, the Claude+Stripe proxy) is NOT DEPLOYED.** No\n123\t  engquest node process runs on the VPS (the `node build/server.js` there is ogbadge). So\n124\t  on the live demo, anything needing the backend **does not function**: AI-graded Writing\n125\t  falls back to its self-check checklist; the planned `/v1/pronounce` (二次 Azure) has no\n126\t  host yet; Stripe checkout is unreachable. Deploying the backend (pm2/systemd/Docker +\n127\t  env keys for Claude/Stripe/Azure) is a prerequisite for those features to work live.\n128\t- **Demo is HTTP-only on a bare IP:8088 — no domain, no HTTPS** (the edilab sites have\n129\t  domains+SSL; engquest does not). Fine for a demo; NOT production-ready. Production needs:\n130\t  a domain + HTTPS, the backend deployed, a real subscriptions DB (the backend currently\n131\t  stores subs in an in-memory Map — lost on restart), and Firebase project config wired.\n132\t- **Firebase:** the app initialises Firebase (main.dart) — the actual Firebase project\n133\t  (Auth/Firestore/Analytics) config + security rules should be documented/verified before\n134\t  launch (not inspected here; flagged).\n135\t\n136\t### Cost model\n137\t- Dev runs within the flat-rate Claude subscription (Claude Code; **no per-token API\n138\t  billing**). Image/voice generation = **local ¥0** (Animagine on the M4 / Kokoro). VPS =\n139\t  existing/shared (no new cost). The ONLY per-usage cost on the horizon: **Azure\n140\t  Pronunciation** for 二次 (~$0.006/min ≈ ~5% of revenue at 5k users) + the production\n141\t  backend's own Claude API calls for writing/二次 grading (rate-limited 10/min/IP,\n142\t  60/hr/UID in server.js). Azure needs an account/key (CEO to provide).\n143\t\n144\t## 7. GOVERNANCE & QUALITY (read docs/governance/QUALITY-CONSTITUTION.md)\n145\t- **R1–R9 enforced rules** + `scripts/verify_quality.sh` (content-integrity / asset-contract\n146\t  / smoke-test audit / leakage grep / analyze / test) — the root-cause fix for \"gates were\n147\t  conventions, never enforced\". Run it before commit/deploy.\n148\t- **Heavy-job guard:** `scripts/safe-job.sh` + a PreToolUse(Bash) hook (`.claude/settings.json`)\n149\t  — ML/gen/long-builds MUST run detached+timeout (a 16-hr hang once occurred). Do NOT modify\n150\t  the guard.\n151\t- **Discipline:** R3 render-proof (smoke test + a `?preview=<screen>` Playwright screenshot\n152\t  LOOKED AT) and R6 evidence-over-claim are mandatory — green unit tests ≠ a working screen\n153\t  (a battle screen once passed 22 tests and rendered a blank crash).\n154\t\n155\t## 8. KNOWN RISKS / DEBT / LAUNCH-BLOCKERS\n156\t- 🔴 **二次 scoring has NO published JP-child-L2 benchmark** → calibrate lenient on real audio\n157\t  in closed beta; treat as formative coaching, not hard pass/fail.\n158\t- 🔴 **保護者同意 consent gate** before any cloud voice send (COPPA-2025 deadline 2026-04-22 /\n159\t  APPI-2026) — simple to build, but a hard LAUNCH BLOCKER. Deferred to pre-launch by CEO.\n160\t- 🟠 backend subscriptions are an in-memory Map (not a real DB) — must persist before billing\n161\t  real customers. RevenueCat (mobile) + Stripe (web) split.\n162\t- 🟠 17 screens lack widget smoke tests (R3 backlog); distractor contextual quality; ¥999↔¥1,480\n163\t  flavor/strategy mismatch; 準2級プラス missing from exam config.\n164\t- 🟠 concurrent CODE agents collide on shared files → use new-files-only scoping or git worktrees.\n165\t\n166\t## 9. KEY DECISION HISTORY (why the product looks like it does)\n167\tMVP (battle-flashcard quiz) → harsh competitive audit scored it 3/10 (\"quiz in a costume\")\n168\t→ pivot to a real RPG verb (サイレントバトル, Wave-1) → CEO rejected as still shallow/dated\n169\t(昭和) → pivot to the **Professor-Layton コトバ探偵** model with full AI-generated world →\n170\tstrategy locked as **Layton soul + Duolingo async engine + total 英検-pass specialization**.\n171\tThe battle is retired front-facing; its FSRS engine powers the spaced-review 館.\n172\t\n173\t## 10. WHAT THE OPUS REVIEWER SHOULD DO (R8 role)\n174\tAdversarially review each work stream BEFORE integration against: (a) this dossier + the\n175\tconstitution; (b) the sole KPI (a kid passes 英検); (c) the premium-calm Layton soul (don't\n176\tlet retention become a guilt treadmill); (d) child-safety/COPPA + Japan minor law; (e)\n177\tsolo-AI-dev sustainability (content-rich single-player, NOT live-ops). Verify by MEASUREMENT\n178\t(run the demo, the gate, read the screenshots), never by claim. Open questions worth a\n179\tsenior call: the ¥999↔¥1,480 reconciliation; the per-word-FSRS + mock + 合格率 efficacy spine;\n180\tthe item-volume scale-up plan; the Azure-vs-self-hosted 二次 timing.\n181\t\n182\t## 11. HOW TO VERIFY (don't trust this doc — measure)\n183\t- Play the demo: `http://178.105.113.79:8088` (Start → placement → コトバ探偵 5級 scene).\n184\t- Run the gate: `bash scripts/verify_quality.sh`. Build: via `scripts/safe-job.sh`.\n185\t- Screens in isolation: `?preview=explore|prologue|placement|writing|listening|questmap|…`.\n186\t- The design canon is in `docs/design/`; the verified 英検 spec in EIKEN-MASTERY-AND-GAPS.json.\n187\t"}]},"uuid":"70e206df-d401-4254-891b-fe178e3316fa","timestamp":"2026-06-06T03:56:28.092Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"f053c541-d332-4796-a601-913a35afa415","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/openclaw/dev/engquest-flutter","sessionId":"e910f43e-613f-4c08-8b89-90096f72c933","version":"2.1.154","gitBranch":"main","slug":"reflective-gliding-newell"}

{"parentUuid":"c684bbff-4d44-40b1-a952-9cf46beaca16","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"41835ea0-fdd5-4fca-9a4b-027790a38dd2","agentId":"afebc93abf2a36b6d","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_016XshVW5zV9RxovJzVBpVvU","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# A-KEN Quest — Quality Constitution (ENFORCED, universal)\n2\t\n3\tEvery recurring defect this project shipped had ONE root cause: a quality gate existed\n4\tas an *intention/convention* but was never **enforced** (automated + blocking). The\n5\tcontent-qa gate \"existed\" yet 10,531 distractors shipped corrupted twice; the asset\n6\tcontract \"existed\" yet silent 🔊s and missing scene art shipped; unit tests were green\n7\twhile screens crashed blank. Conventions don't hold. Only **enforced gates** hold.\n8\t\n9\tThese rules are MANDATORY, apply to EVERY change and EVERY agent, and are enforced by\n10\t`scripts/verify_quality.sh` (run pre-commit / CI). A change that fails any gate is NOT\n11\tdone. \"Done/fixed/works\" is a measurement, never a claim.\n12\t\n13\t## R1 — CONTENT INTEGRITY (enforced: content-integrity test)\n14\tNo learning item may ship with: mixed-language options (an option set is uniformly EN\n15\tor uniformly JP per the item's format), ≠1 correct answer, duplicate options, an option\n16\tequal to the answer-key derivation, or wrong-type distractors. Applies to ALL content\n17\t(vocab JSONs, quest_data, exam items, placement bank). The 英検 answer/distractors are\n18\tnever altered by a flavour/framing change.\n19\t\n20\t## R2 — ASSET CONTRACT (enforced: asset-contract test)\n21\tEvery asset key referenced in code or data (autoPlayAudio, audioAsset, imageAsset,\n22\tbackgroundAsset, parallaxLayers, npc*Asset, scene plates…) MUST resolve to a real file,\n23\tOR be listed in an explicit `ALLOWED_MISSING` registry with a dated reason (e.g. the 6\n24\tfounder-recorded phonemes). A silent errorBuilder/no-op fallback must NEVER mask a\n25\tmissing asset that the design assumes exists. New referenced asset → file or registry.\n26\t\n27\t## R3 — RENDER PROOF (enforced: smoke-test audit + screenshot discipline)\n28\tA screen is NOT done without BOTH: (a) a widget SMOKE TEST that pumps it and asserts\n29\t`tester.takeException() == null`; and (b) a real screenshot LOOKED AT (a `?preview=`\n30\troute + Playwright). Green unit tests ≠ a working screen (proven: the battle passed 22\n31\tunit tests and rendered a blank crash). Measure, don't assume.\n32\t\n33\t## R4 — NO FIREBASE / NETWORK / HEAVY WORK IN BUILD OR INIT\n34\tWidgets must render with NO Firebase/network dependency. Persistence and side-effects\n35\tare lazy, guarded (try/catch), and fired from gestures/lifecycle-after-first-frame, not\n36\tfield initializers or initState. (Root cause of the battle blank-crash: eager\n37\t`AuthService()`/`XpService()` hit `Firebase.instance` at init.)\n38\t\n39\t## R5 — CHANGE COMPLETENESS (enforced: leakage grep)\n40\tA wholesale content/narrative/rename change ends with a repo-wide grep proving ZERO\n41\tresidue (e.g. `魔王|王子|王女|prince|heir` after the restoration recast). Half-shipped =\n42\tself-contradiction on screen. The grep is part of the gate, not a hope.\n43\t\n44\t## R6 — EVIDENCE OVER CLAIM (operator rule)\n45\tMemory, prior outputs, and assumptions are LEADS, not proof. When correctness matters,\n46\tverify with current direct evidence: actual files, logs, test output, screenshots, HTTP\n47\tcodes, real API/system output. Conflicting evidence → current verified evidence wins.\n48\tSeparate verified-fact / assumption / estimate. Never present speculation as fact.\n49\t\n50\t## R7 — AGENT BRIEFING (operator rule)\n51\tNo agent works from stale/isolated/incomplete context. Every agent brief includes:\n52\tobjective, facts, verified evidence, constraints, assumptions, uncertainties, risks,\n53\tdependencies, required output, THESE RULES, and the live-research mandate (cite dated\n54\t2025–2026 sources; treat training priors AND existing code as stale-until-verified).\n55\t\n56\t## R8 — OPUS INTERVENTION (operator rule)\n57\tA top-tier (Opus) reviewer reviews, corrects, and may redirect each parallel stream's\n58\toutput BEFORE integration — adversarially, against this constitution + the real-world\n59\tobjective (a kid passes 英検; premium calm Layton immersion; subscription retention;\n60\tchild-safety/COPPA; Japan minor-monetization law). Integration waits on that review.\n61\t\n62\t## R9 — SCOPE & SOUL GUARDS\n63\t- The moat is TOTAL 英検-pass specialization; every feature must serve \"a kid passes 英検.\"\n64\t- The form is Layton soul + gentle async Duolingo retention; NO gacha, real-time PvP,\n65\t  kid chat, energy/heart paywalls, or child-buyable currency (law + parent-trust + the\n66\t  \"premium calm\" differentiation). Retention must be diegetic and gentle.\n67\t- AI generation stays inside one cohesive storybook style; never ship obvious AI slop.\n68\t\n69\t## Enforcement\n70\t\n71\t`scripts/verify_quality.sh` (bash, exits non-zero on any hard failure) runs:\n72\t\n73\t| Check | Rule | Hard-fail? | Implementation |\n74\t|-------|------|-----------|----------------|\n75\t| Vocab distractor integrity | R1 | yes | `scripts/qa/check_content_integrity.py` |\n76\t| Quest data structural correctness | R1 | yes (via flutter test) | `test/features/quest/quest_data_test.dart` |\n77\t| Asset contract | R2 | yes (unregistered missing) | `scripts/qa/check_asset_contract.py` |\n78\t| Smoke-test presence | R3 | warn-only v1 (set HARD_FAIL=1) | `scripts/qa/check_smoke_tests.py` |\n79\t| Narrative leakage grep | R5 | yes | inline grep in verify_quality.sh |\n80\t| flutter analyze | — | yes | `flutter analyze` |\n81\t| flutter test | — | yes | `flutter test` |\n82\t\n83\t**Asset exceptions registry**: `assets/ALLOWED_MISSING.txt` — listed missing assets\n84\tare WARN (not hard-fail). Requires a dated reason. Remove entries when files land.\n85\t\n86\t**Usage**:\n87\t```bash\n88\t./scripts/verify_quality.sh            # full gate (R1/R2/R3/R5 + analyze + test)\n89\t./scripts/verify_quality.sh --fast     # R1/R2/R3/R5 only (skip analyze/test)\n90\tHARD_FAIL=1 ./scripts/verify_quality.sh  # promote R3 smoke-test gaps to hard fail\n91\t```\n92\t\n93\t**Wiring**: Run as pre-commit and pre-deploy gate. A failing gate blocks the change.\n94\tThe `.claude/settings.json` `PreToolUse` Bash hook (`guard-heavy-jobs.sh`) is separate\n95\tand must NOT be modified — it guards against heavy job hangs; this gate guards content\n96\tand code quality.\n97\t"}]},"uuid":"dee9cc48-59b1-4f0d-8dc0-6ab96c0077ca","timestamp":"2026-06-06T03:56:28.531Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"c684bbff-4d44-40b1-a952-9cf46beaca16","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/openclaw/dev/engquest-flutter","sessionId":"e910f43e-613f-4c08-8b89-90096f72c933","version":"2.1.154","gitBranch":"main","slug":"reflective-gliding-newell"}

Let me verify the explore feature code and the pass meter logic."}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":3160,"cache_read_input_tokens":31447,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":3160,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"diagnostics":null},"requestId":"req_011CbmLHVstW7BpihAJyzPH4","attributionAgent":"general-purpose","type":"assistant","uuid":"dac99e61-b88d-4ef9-8a11-318394b66454","timestamp":"2026-06-06T03:56:43.046Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/openclaw/dev/engquest-flutter","sessionId":"e910f43e-613f-4c08-8b89-90096f72c933","version":"2.1.154","gitBranch":"main","slug":"reflective-gliding-newell"}
{"parentUuid":"dac99e61-b88d-4ef9-8a11-318394b66454","isSidechain":true,"agentId":"afebc93abf2a36b6d","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-8","id":"msg_01Kan3NQ8txNMs7MHfdBtjrA","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01CQpF9sLopYATAMmxix11qm","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"ls lib/features/explore/ lib/features/exam_practice/ lib/features/exam_practice/pass/ lib/features/speaking/ 2>/dev/null","description":"List explore, exam_practice, pass, speaking feature dirs"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":3160,"cache_read_input_tokens":31447,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":3160,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"diagnostics":null},"requestId":"req_011CbmLHVstW7BpihAJyzPH4","attributionAgent":"general-purpose","type":"assistant","uuid":"57a1db2e-7005-4784-b3c0-d2a0ea86f435","timestamp":"2026-06-06T03:56:44.934Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/openclaw/dev/engquest-flutter","sessionId":"e910f43e-613f-4c08-8b89-90096f72c933","version":"2.1.154","gitBranch":"main","slug":"reflective-gliding-newell"}
{"parentUuid":"57a1db2e-7005-4784-b3c0-d2a0ea86f435","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"41835ea0-fdd5-4fca-9a4b-027790a38dd2","agentId":"afebc93abf2a36b6d","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01CQpF9sLopYATAMmxix11qm","type":"tool_result","content":"lib/features/exam_practice/:\nconversation_practice_screen.dart\neiken_exam_config.dart\nexam_practice_screen.dart\nlistening_data.dart\nlistening_practice_screen.dart\npass\nreading_practice_screen.dart\nvocab_grammar_practice_screen.dart\nword_ordering_practice_screen.dart\nwriting_practice_screen.dart\n\nlib/features/exam_practice/pass/:\ncse_model.dart\nmock_exam.dart\npass_meter_screen.dart\nreading_item_pool.dart\n\nlib/features/explore/:\nhotspot.dart\nnazo_screen.dart\nscene_view.dart\n\nlib/features/speaking/:\npronunciation_scorer.dart\nspeaking_consent_notice.dart\nspeaking_screen.dart\nspeaking_session.dart","is_error":false}]},"uuid":"2488b1b0-6109-417f-bdfb-3372466f1055","timestamp":"2026-06-06T03:56:45.010Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"57a1db2e-7005-4784-b3c0-d2a0ea86f435","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/openclaw/dev/engquest-flutter","sessionId":"e910f43e-613f-4c08-8b89-90096f72c933","version":"2.1.154","gitBranch":"main","slug":"reflective-gliding-newell"}
{"parentUuid":"2488b1b0-6109-417f-bdfb-3372466f1055","isSidechain":true,"agentId":"afebc93abf2a36b6d","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-8","id":"msg_01Kan3NQ8txNMs7MHfdBtjrA","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_012Am3YfX4H4khZhMoXZQBwV","name":"Read","input":{"file_path":"/Users/openclaw/dev/engquest-flutter/docs/design/STRATEGY-LAYTON-VS-HYBRID-2026-06-05.json"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":3160,"cache_read_input_tokens":31447,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":3160,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"diagnostics":null},"requestId":"req_011CbmLHVstW7BpihAJyzPH4","attributionAgent":"general-purpose","type":"assistant","uuid":"571a2afb-1116-419f-b00c-befbcfa91010","timestamp":"2026-06-06T03:56:45.506Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/openclaw/dev/engquest-flutter","sessionId":"e910f43e-613f-4c08-8b89-90096f72c933","version":"2.1.154","gitBranch":"main","slug":"reflective-gliding-newell"}
{"parentUuid":"571a2afb-1116-419f-b00c-befbcfa91010","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"41835ea0-fdd5-4fca-9a4b-027790a38dd2","agentId":"afebc93abf2a36b6d","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_012Am3YfX4H4khZhMoXZQBwV","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t{\n2\t  \"summary\": \"Expert opinion: should A-KEN Quest stay pure-Layton or go hybrid? Deep 2026 online-game research → an opinionated, dated recommendation\",\n3\t  \"agentCount\": 5,\n4\t  \"logs\": [\n5\t    \"4 experts mastering the 2026 online-game / monetization / AI-dev / Layton-limits landscape…\",\n6\t    \"Senior director forming the blunt recommendation…\"\n7\t  ],\n8\t  \"result\": {\n9\t    \"verdict\": \"Go HYBRID — but a specific, narrow one I'll call \\\"LAYTON SHELL + DUOLINGO ENGINE\\\": keep the painted/voiced/AI-generated コトバ探偵 world as the premium single-player SOUL, and bolt on ONLY Duolingo's async, server-light, gacha-free retention spine (streak → daily FSRS ナゾ, async cohort/family leaderboards, parent 英検-progress loop, monthly AI-generated episode cadence) — and explicitly REJECT the 英語物語/マグナ live-PvP-and-gacha hybrid.\",\n10\t    \"why\": \"The CEO's framing is a false binary. The real question isn't \\\"Layton vs hybrid,\\\" it's \\\"which retention layer\\\" — because A-KEN is a ¥1,480/mo SUBSCRIPTION where retention literally is the business, and the two extremes both fail it.\\n\\nPURE-LAYTON FAILS THE BUSINESS MODEL. Layton is a one-time premium PURCHASE (the new entry, New World of Steam, is a buy-once console/PC title delayed to 2026 — verified) with a finish line and sequels every 2-3 years. A subscription needs a day-2/day-30 return reason a finite narrative structurally lacks. Kids-edu apps churn ~7.4%/mo and the #1 cause is the child disengaging (36%) plus content age-out (24%) — a beatable story hits both walls. The killer proof point I verified live: Recruit's スタディサプリ ENGLISH for KIDS — a deep-pocketed premium kids-English subscription, the closest analog to A-KEN — is being SHUT DOWN 2026-06-30. Premium-only just died in this exact niche.\\n\\nTHE FULL GACHA/SOCIAL HYBRID ALSO FAILS — on three independent axes. (1) Unbuildable by an AI-solo dev: 英語物語's 10-player raids and マグナ's 50-player live battles need servers, matchmaking, anti-cheat, 24/7 minor-moderation, and a weekly live cadence AI does NOT generate (2026 live-ops consensus: AI's value is backend tooling, not player-facing live content; 52 live-service games already shut down by mid-2026). (2) Regulatory/parent-trust minefield for minors in Japan: コンプガチャ banned since 2012, 2024 amendments tightened randomized-monetization-for-minors, and I verified Apple's Dec-2025 Japan rules now force a parental gate on under-18 purchases and block under-13 web checkout — the direction is harder, not easier. (3) Obsolete even among incumbents: Mintflag (the 英検-gacha leader) is publicly pivoting AWAY from gacha-as-revenue and runs マグナ as permanently FREE (verified 400k users, 400+ schools). You cannot out-gacha or out-social a free voiced anime RPG, and you shouldn't try.\\n\\nWHAT WINS is the synthesis the whole market is converging on. Duolingo's Q1-2026 numbers (verified from SEC filing: 56.5M DAU +21% YoY, 12.5M paid subs, 10M+ users on 1yr+ streaks, ONE-THIRD of DAUs on a Friend Streak) prove the retention engine is async streaks + leagues + family plan — NOT gacha, NOT real-time PvP, NOT narrative. Every one of those primitives is single-player/async: zero servers-as-realtime, zero kid-chat moderation, COPPA/parent-safe. And the Layton soul is precisely what an AI-full-driven solo team is BEST at mass-producing at ¥0 (endless generatable 英検 puzzles + cohesive storybook world). So: hybrid in OUTCOME (you get the daily-return engine pure-Layton fatally lacks), Layton in SOUL (the calm premium immersion the CEO loves and AI makes cheap), solo-AI-sustainable in BUILD (content, not live-ops). The sole KPI — a kid actually passes 英検 — is served by making the daily habit literally BE the FSRS spaced review, surfaced as 今日のナゾ, with the parent dashboard proving readiness. That's the renewal trigger, not a loot box.\",\n11\t    \"hybridGraftRanked\": [\n12\t      \"1. STREAK + daily ナゾ quota — the single highest-ROI, cheapest retention lever (Duolingo: 10M+ users on 1yr+ streaks). Pure client + Firestore, zero servers. This IS your daily-return engine. Make the streak action = solving today's NPC ナゾ.\",\n13\t      \"2. FSRS 復習の館 reframed as the streak destination / 今日のナゾ — you already built FSRS; spaced review IS the scientifically-grounded daily habit AND the 英検-pass mechanism. Lean into it as the engagement CORE, not a side feature. This is the rare lever where retention and the sole KPI are the same action.\",\n14\t      \"3. PARENT DASHBOARD with 英検-readiness gauge + 'your child passed a mock 級' milestone — the 2-3x retention multiplier for kids subs, the conversion/renewal trigger, and the proof of sole value. The parent is the payer; make them the audience. Non-negotiable.\",\n15\t      \"4. MONTHLY AI-generated episode/town cadence (Animagine XL, ¥0) — this is 'live-ops delivered as story DLC' with NO live server. Solves the finite-narrative age-out churn hole and turns your AI pipeline into an unfair content-cadence advantage.\",\n16\t      \"5. MULTI-GRADE progression 5級→準1級 (you have it) as celebrated narrative milestones — the 3-5x LTV anti-age-out lever. Grade-up = a big in-world event so content never 'ends.'\",\n17\t      \"6. ASYNC, SAFE social only: ghost/cohort leaderboards by ピカラット, Duolingo-style Friend/Family Streak, class/family progress feed, shareable solved-case cards. Captures the league effect (one-third of Duolingo DAU use Friend Streak) with ZERO live chat, PvP, or moderation burden. Family-shaped, since the family plan is Duolingo's fastest-growing SKU.\",\n18\t      \"7. ひらめきコイン / ピカラット as EARNED-ONLY currency, never purchasable — cosmetics unlocked by play, never by chance. Keeps you clear of 射幸心/gacha regulation while still giving a reward economy.\"\n19\t    ],\n20\t    \"avoid\": [\n21\t      \"GACHA / paid loot boxes / collectible-card-by-chance monetization — コンプガチャ banned, 2024 minor-monetization scrutiny, Apple Dec-2025 parental-gate rules, and the incumbent 英語物語 itself is fleeing it. Disproportionate legal+PR risk for a ¥1,480 sub, and it torches the parent-trust that is your actual moat.\",\n22\t      \"Real-time co-op raids / live PvP / 50-player live battles (英語物語/マグナ style) — require servers, matchmaking, anti-cheat, 24/7 minor moderation, and a relentless live content cadence a solo AI dev cannot staff. AI does not generate the hard parts of live-ops. And you can't out-social a FREE voiced anime RPG anyway.\",\n23\t      \"Open kid-to-kid chat / social-media-style features — rising G7/Japan minor-safety + age-assurance burden; moderation liability with no upside for a learning app.\",\n24\t      \"Aggressive energy/heart paywall hooks (Duolingo's 2025 Energy backlash — ~3,000-upvote revolt) — dark patterns that punish even correct answers will destroy the parent goodwill that drives word-of-mouth in the kids category.\",\n25\t      \"Any IAP currency a child can buy — keep it ONE flat subscription, no consumables, no in-app purchasable currency. Simplicity is the trust signal and the only model a solo AI team can sustain.\",\n26\t      \"Don't splash visible AI across social/multiplayer surfaces — keep AI generation inside the cohesive storybook frame (where the team already excels); GDC 2026 shows rising backlash to obvious AI assets, but a unified painted world reads as craft, not slop.\"\n27\t    ],\n28\t    \"biggestRisk\": \"The biggest risk is DILUTION OF THE PREMIUM CALM — that grafting streaks, leaderboards and 'today's quota' onto Layton recreates a Duolingo-style anxiety/guilt treadmill and kills the very immersion that is A-KEN's differentiation (and the thing AI uniquely lets you produce at quality). Layton's brand is calm, curious, unhurried; streak-loss-aversion is its tonal opposite. Mitigation: make the retention layer DIEGETIC and gentle — the streak is a 探偵's case-log, not a red 'you'll lose it!' nag; no energy/hearts wall ever; FSRS review framed as 'a new ナゾ arrived at the 館,' not a chore meter. Tune for parent-trust and intrinsic mastery over loss-aversion. If you must err, err calm: a slightly weaker streak that preserves the premium feel beats an aggressive one that makes A-KEN feel like every other guilt app — because the calm IS the product.\",\n29\t    \"nextWaves\": \"Concretely for the build waves (all single-player/async — nothing here needs a live server, matchmaking, or moderation): WAVE 2 = retention spine. Wire the daily STREAK to FSRS-due reviews surfaced as 今日のナゾ from an NPC (this is the one feature with highest leverage and you already have FSRS). Ship the PARENT DASHBOARD with a 英検-readiness gauge + mock-exam milestone (this is your conversion/renewal trigger and proof of the sole KPI — treat it as P0, not parent-facing afterthought). Confirm flat ¥1,480 sub with NO IAP currency. WAVE 3 = content cadence + anti-age-out. Stand up the AI episode pipeline to ship one new town/case per MONTH (your no-server live-ops), and make 5級→準1級 grade-ups celebrated in-world events. WAVE 4 = async social, last. Add Friend/Family Streak + ghost/cohort leaderboards by ピカラット + shareable solved-case cards — async only, no chat. EXPLICITLY OFF THE ROADMAP (write it down so it doesn't creep back in): gacha, paid loot, real-time co-op/PvP, energy/heart paywalls, open chat. Resolve the existing audio-asset contract (T35) and child-safety crisis gap (per project memory: a suicidal-child scold path and in-memory billing are launch blockers) BEFORE adding any social surface — trust infrastructure precedes engagement features for a paid kids app. Spec-freeze this hybrid shape now per your own anti-churn governance: the design has flipped 4x; this is the one that matches both the market data and what your team can actually sustain — stop re-litigating it and build.\",\n30\t    \"sources\": [\n31\t      {\n32\t        \"title\": \"Duolingo Q1 FY2026 Form 8-K (SEC) — 56.5M DAU, 12.5M paid subs, 1/3 of DAU on Friend Streak\",\n33\t        \"url\": \"https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/0001562088/000162828026029790/q1fy26duolingo3-31x26share.htm\",\n34\t        \"date\": \"2026-05\"\n35\t      },\n36\t      {\n37\t        \"title\": \"Duolingo Q4 FY2025 Form 8-K (SEC) — 52.7M DAU +30% YoY, 12.2M paid subs, 10M+ users on 1yr+ streaks\",\n38\t        \"url\": \"https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/0001562088/000162828026012246/q4fy25duolingo12-31x25shar.htm\",\n39\t        \"date\": \"2026-02\"\n40\t      },\n41\t      {\n42\t        \"title\": \"『スタディサプリENGLISH for KIDS』サービス終了のお知らせ — 株式会社リクルート\",\n43\t        \"url\": \"https://www.recruit.co.jp/newsroom/pressrelease/2025/0407_15613.html\",\n44\t        \"date\": \"2025-04-07\"\n45\t      },\n46\t      {\n47\t        \"title\": \"AIえいご-マグナとふしぎの少女 ずっと無料の英語ゲーム — Google Play (400k users, 400+ schools, permanently free)\",\n48\t        \"url\": \"https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?hl=en_US&id=com.mintflag.magna\",\n49\t        \"date\": \"2026\"\n50\t      },\n51\t      {\n52\t        \"title\": \"学校版『マグナとふしぎの少女』無償AI英語アプリ — MINTFLAG (free + school-adoption growth model, no gacha)\",\n53\t        \"url\": \"https://mintflag.com/education\",\n54\t        \"date\": \"2026\"\n55\t      },\n56\t      {\n57\t        \"title\": \"Apple announces changes to iOS in Japan — Apple Newsroom (under-18 parental gate, under-13 no web checkout, eff. Dec 18 2025)\",\n58\t        \"url\": \"https://www.apple.com/newsroom/2025/12/apple-announces-changes-to-ios-in-japan/\",\n59\t        \"date\": \"2025-12-17\"\n60\t      },\n61\t      {\n62\t        \"title\": \"Kids Education App Churn Rate 2026: 7.4% Monthly Benchmark — RetentionCheck\",\n63\t        \"url\": \"https://retentioncheck.com/churn-benchmarks/kids-education-apps\",\n64\t        \"date\": \"2026\"\n65\t      },\n66\t      {\n67\t        \"title\": \"Duolingo Gamification Strategy: A Full Case Study (2026) — Trophy.so\",\n68\t        \"url\": \"https://trophy.so/blog/duolingo-gamification-case-study\",\n69\t        \"date\": \"2026\"\n70\t      },\n71\t      {\n72\t        \"title\": \"Duolingo Breaks Hearts for Energy, Drains Free Learning — Class Central (2025 Energy backlash)\",\n73\t        \"url\": \"https://www.classcentral.com/report/duolingo-breaks-hearts-for-energy/\",\n74\t        \"date\": \"2025\"\n75\t      },\n76\t      {\n77\t        \"title\": \"52 Games Have Already Been Shut Down in 2026 — Game Rant (live-service graveyard)\",\n78\t        \"url\": \"https://gamerant.com/games-shut-down-2026-list-highguard-anthem/\",\n79\t        \"date\": \"2026\"\n80\t      },\n81\t      {\n82\t        \"title\": \"2026 live ops trends: Templatisation, personalisation and AI — PocketGamer.biz (AI = backend tooling, not player-facing live content)\",\n83\t        \"url\": \"https://www.pocketgamer.biz/2026-live-ops-trends-templatisation-personalisation-and-ai/\",\n84\t        \"date\": \"2026\"\n85\t      },\n86\t      {\n87\t        \"title\": \"Legal Regulation of Game Gacha in Japan — Lexology (kompu-gacha banned 2012; 2024 minor-monetization scrutiny)\",\n88\t        \"url\": \"https://www.lexology.com/library/detail.aspx?g=9207df10-a8a2-4f67-81c3-6a148a6100e2\",\n89\t        \"date\": \"2025\"\n90\t      },\n91\t      {\n92\t        \"title\": \"オンラインゲームの課金トラブルにご注意ください — 消費者庁 (minors' purchases voidable; 子供の射幸心 scrutiny)\",\n93\t        \"url\": \"https://www.caa.go.jp/policies/policy/consumer_policy/caution/caution_022/\",\n94\t        \"date\": \"2024\"\n95\t      },\n96\t      {\n97\t        \"title\": \"Professor Layton and the New World of Steam delayed until 2026 — Nintendo Life (premium one-time purchase, no live-ops by design)\",\n98\t        \"url\": \"https://www.nintendolife.com/news/2025/09/professor-layton-and-the-new-world-of-steam-has-been-delayed-until-2026\",\n99\t        \"date\": \"2025-09-25\"\n100\t      },\n101\t      {\n102\t        \"title\": \"教科化で広がる英検チャレンジ、小学生の準2級受験者が10年で2倍 — ReseEd (375,991 小学生 takers FY2024; growing demand)\",\n103\t        \"url\": \"https://reseed.resemom.jp/article/2025/08/20/11524.html\",\n104\t        \"date\": \"2025-08-20\"\n105\t      }\n106\t    ]\n107\t  }\n108\t}"}]},"uuid":"7d2b0c58-0a40-44e2-baff-180fdc40791f","timestamp":"2026-06-06T03:56:45.865Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"571a2afb-1116-419f-b00c-befbcfa91010","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/openclaw/dev/engquest-flutter","sessionId":"e910f43e-613f-4c08-8b89-90096f72c933","version":"2.1.154","gitBranch":"main","slug":"reflective-gliding-newell"}
